preamble: The short vowel /a/ reduces to schwa [ə] in unstressed or final syllables across multiple spoken Arabic dialects. This vowel reduction process is particularly prominent in Moroccan and other Maghrebi varieties where short vowels undergo systematic neutralization.

examples.note: Short /a/ merges with schwa in unstressed syllables across Moroccan Arabic phonology
Closed syllable shortening triggers vowel reduction in Egyptian Arabic

context: Unstressed or final syllables in spoken Arabic dialects; word-final and non-prominence positions
allophones_note: Vowel reduction in unstressed positions across multiple Arabic varieties
